submissionUrl: https://smithery.ai/new (web form - requires browser/JS rendering) | https://api.smithery.ai (API - requires auth) | CLI: smithery mcp publish <url> -n <namespace>/<server>
message:
Could not complete submission. Obstructions documented below.

Obstructions:
1. AUTHENTICATION REQUIRED: Smithery requires an interactive browser-based OAuth login flow. The CLI outputs an auth_url that must be opened in a browser to authenticate (non-headless environment not supported). The API requires a Bearer token (API key) in Authorization header.

2. WEB FORM UNAVAILABLE: The web form at https://smithery.ai/new returns a 404 error page when accessed from a non-JS client. The site is a Next.js single-page application requiring JavaScript rendering, so the submission form cannot be accessed via curl.

3. PROTOCOL MISMATCH: Smithery is exclusively an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server marketplace. It accepts MCP servers using Streamable HTTP transport (for URL-based servers) or stdio MCPB bundles (for local servers). 'my-automaton' as described ('AI code review, security scanning, and text analysis via REST API') is a REST API, not an MCP server. For listing on Smithery, the service would need to implement the MCP protocol specification.

4. NO PUBLIC SUBMISSION FORM: There is no publicly accessible form, GitHub issue template, or contact form for submitting agents/servers without authentication.

Submission Process Research:
- Registry URL: https://registry.smithery.ai (returns 404)
- API: https://api.smithery.ai (health check: OK)
- Web: https://smithery.ai (main site)
- Docs: https://smithery.ai/docs/build/publish
- CLI: smithery mcp publish <target> -n <org>/<name>
  - Supports URL-based (public HTTPS MCP endpoint)
  - Supports bundle-based (MCPB file for stdio servers)
- API: PUT /servers/{qualifiedName} (create server), PUT /servers/{qualifiedName}/releases (publish release)

Prepared Description (for reference):
"AI code review, security scanning, and text analysis via REST API"

Service URL: localhost:8080 (would need a public HTTPS URL for Smithery listing)

Suggested Next Steps:
1. Create a Smithery account at https://smithery.ai
2. Wrap the existing REST API with an MCP protocol interface (implement Streamable HTTP transport)
3. Deploy the MCP server to a public HTTPS endpoint
4. Run: smithery mcp publish https://your-public-mcp-url -n your-namespace/my-automaton
5. Or use the web form at https://smithery.ai/new (in browser)
